title: terebinthus cinerea
intro: Terebinthus cinerea, also known as the Aleppo pine or Candole pine, is a species of evergreen tree native to the Mediterranean region. It is a tree of medicinal importance, containing a resin called turpentine, which possesses antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The resin and other parts of the tree have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, skin conditions, and digestive issues, offering benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. The herbal preparations of Terebinthus cinerea include essential oils, ointments, and infusions, which are often used in aromatherapy and as natural remedies.
